records.ety: [For shoal a crowd; prob. confused with school for learning.]

defs: A shoal; a multitude; as, a school of fish.

records.ety: [OE. scole, AS. sc<?/lu, L. schola, Gr. <?/ leisure, that in which leisure is employed, disputation, lecture, a school, probably from the same root as <?/, the original sense being perhaps, a stopping, a resting. See Scheme.]

defs: A place for learned intercourse and instruction; an institution for learning; an educational establishment; a place for acquiring knowledge and mental training; as, the school of the prophets.
quotes: Disputing daily in the school of one Tyrannus.

defs: A place of primary instruction; an establishment for the instruction of children; as, a primary school; a common school; a grammar school.
quotes: As he sat in the school at his primer.

defs: A session of an institution of instruction.
quotes: How now, Sir Hugh! No school to-day?

defs: One of the seminaries for teaching logic, metaphysics, and theology, which were formed in the Middle Ages, and which were characterized by academical disputations and subtleties of reasoning.
quotes: At Cambridge the philosophy of Descartes was still dominant in the schools.

defs: The room or hall in English universities where the examinations for degrees and honors are held.
An assemblage of scholars; those who attend upon instruction in a school of any kind; a body of pupils.
quotes: What is the great community of Christians, but one of the innumerable schools in the vast plan which God has instituted for the education of various intelligences?

defs: The disciples or followers of a teacher; those who hold a common doctrine, or accept the same teachings; a sect or denomination in philosophy, theology, science, medicine, politics, etc.
quotes: Let no man be less confident in his faith . . . by reason of any difference in the several schools of Christians.

defs: The canons, precepts, or body of opinion or practice, sanctioned by the authority of a particular class or age; as, he was a gentleman of the old school.
quotes: His face pale but striking, though not handsome after the schools.

defs: Figuratively, any means of knowledge or discipline; as, the school of experience.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Schooled (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Schooling.]

defs: To train in an institution of learning; to educate at a school; to teach.
quotes: He's gentle, never schooled, and yet learned.

defs: To tutor; to chide and admonish; to reprove; to subject to systematic discipline; to train.
quotes: It now remains for you to school your child, And ask why God's Anointed be reviled.
The mother, while loving her child with the intensity of a sole affection, had schooled herself to hope for little other return than the waywardness of an April breeze.
